# First Frame, Hooks, Text, Faces, and Story Tactics

## First-frame tactics

The first frame should answer:

```text
What is this?
Who is it for?
Why should I care?
```

### First-frame hierarchy

| First-frame type | Best for | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Before/after split | visual transformation | bad caption vs good caption |
| Result first | tutorials, edits, builds | final edited clip shown immediately |
| Visible problem | education, critique | caption covering subject’s face |
| Data/proof | authority content | retention graph, analytics screenshot |
| Face + emotion | trust/personality | creator reacting to mistake |
| Screen close-up | software/tutorial | zoomed timeline or UI problem |
| Object close-up | product/process | object already in action |
| Unusual visual | entertainment/curiosity | unexpected movement or setup |

### Rules

```text
one dominant subject
show problem or result before explanation
readable without sound
no clutter in first frame
```

## Hook tactics

A hook is a specific unresolved tension.

| Hook type | Template | Use when |
|---|---|---|
| Problem-first | “This is why people swipe in the first 2 seconds.” | fixing mistakes |
| Result-first | “This one caption move makes the clip clearer.” | tutorials |
| Contrarian | “Captions are not enough. Placement matters more.” | myth-busting |
| Proof-first | “Here’s the retention drop from this edit.” | trust/authority |
| Experiment | “I tested 3 hooks. One failed instantly.” | completion |
| Identity | “Every editor has made this mistake.” | sends/comments |
| Cost-of-inaction | “This makes your video feel amateur.” | retention |
| Challenge | “Can I fix this boring clip in 20 seconds?” | entertainment + education |
| Before/after | “Same clip. Two edits. Different result.” | transformation |
| Search-direct | “How to place captions on Reels without covering the subject.” | long-tail search |

### Hook scoring

Score out of 10:

```text
Specific problem: /2
Clear target viewer: /2
Tension or curiosity: /2
Visualisable immediately: /2
Accurate payoff: /2
```

Reject below 8.

## Text tactics

Text jobs:

```text
hook headline
caption/subtitle
section marker
comparison label
proof label
annotation
keyword label
CTA
```

### Rules

```text
Use captions for speech-heavy videos.
Use labels for visual comparisons.
Use annotations when the viewer might miss the key detail.
Use navigation text in multi-step videos.
Avoid huge central text over dynamic footage.
Change text at story beats, not randomly.
```

## Face tactics

Face is an attention and trust anchor.

Use face for:

```text
trust
emotion
judgment
personality
authority
human reaction
```

Do not overuse face when the viewer needs:

```text
screen detail
product detail
before/after comparison
process visibility
tiny UI inspection
```

Best formats:

```text
face open → proof insert
face-cam over screen
reaction face + example
face only for hook and conclusion
no face + strong proof
```

## Story tactics

Use:

```text
before state → tension → intervention → visible change → takeaway
```

Strong structures:

```text
problem → test → result
before → after → why
myth → evidence → correction
teardown
checklist
challenge
diagnostic
```

Timing:

```text
0–1s: visual signal
1–3s: hook / tension
3–7s: first payoff
7–80%: proof, steps, contrast, escalation
final 20%: result, rule, CTA, loop
```
